intro: Gongora latibasis, also known as the "hidden hat orchid," is a rare and unique species of orchid native to South America, characterized by its distinctive, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gongora latibasis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to make remedies such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use is largely anecdotal and further scientific research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
